source_item.title: Manipal Hospitals is said to cut IPO valuation from $10-12 billion estimated earlier

slide1_hook.label: India's biggest hospital IPO just got cheaper
slide1_hook.subhead: Manipal trims valuation to $8.3 billion from $10-12 billion

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: From one medical college to 38 hospitals
slide2_backstory.body: Manipal grew from one Karnataka college into India's biggest private hospital chain, with over 10,700 beds. Temasek bought a majority stake in 2023 at a $5 billion valuation.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: Valuation cut to $8.3 billion amid volatility
slide3_news.body: Manipal now wants a valuation of about Rs 80,000 crore, or $8.3 billion, down from the $10-12 billion range floated earlier. It still plans to raise up to Rs 11,000 crore and could list as early as the week of July 27.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: Still India's biggest listing of the year
bullets: Still set to beat SBI Funds as 2026's biggest IPO
Proceeds will pay down debt and fund expansion
Lower price could mean cheaper entry for investors

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: Is an $8.3 billion valuation still too rich for a hospital chain, or a bargain after the cut?
slide5_engage.cta: Drop your view in the comments and follow for more IPO breakdowns
